All right, so YouTube is a totally different animal than Facebook like night and day difference Good thing about YouTube is people are generally more Intent based on YouTube. So if you think about Facebook advertising for example or Instagram, we're always interrupting them, right? Like they're just screwing around when someone's on YouTube They're either like just randomly watching videos, which is you know, sometimes the norm or they're looking for stuff You know like for tutorials or TED talks or whatever and what's really cool is Google, which of course is YouTube they've got unbelievable data skills. So they know everything about everybody. It's unbelievable. So our targeting abilities on YouTube are fantastic. All right, and we can target people Within specific countries, there's a little limitations to this, but we can target people by income We can target people by what sites they're going to we can target people by what videos they watch by what keywords they search for Even things that YouTube Google will figure out on their own like whether or not Based on this person's behavior, they're in the market for the very type of stuff that you sell. I mean, it's just like Phenomenal now with all of that said is a totally different animal. All right, so it's typically more expensive Generally speaking like some you know, maybe you can crack the code and get cheaper leads or whatever on YouTube than Facebook But generally speaking it's more expensive. The other side of that coin is that generally speaking it's better So the traffic is a higher caliber of traffic because especially if you do your targeting right and stuff and your ads good There's so much intent there. All right What this means TCPA is it means target cost per acquisition? This is where I'm telling Google how much I'm willing to pay For a sale. It's similar to cost cap bidding in Facebook But as you can see I've bid here 70 bucks. I'm like the heck with it I'll pay $70 an opt-in and the reason why is because on average I was getting them for $15 So it doesn't mean you have to pay 70s Like you just bid up that much and then you see what they can get it for you know, so it's kind of a weird strategy There's multiple bidding strategies by the way I'll go over them with you in this video.
